name: ask-me-copilot-tool
displayName: Ask Me Copilot Tool - Expert Collaboration for GitHub Copilot
description: Transform GitHub Copilot into a collaborative partner by enabling interactive expert communication. Get asked for input when AI needs guidance, preventing errors and improving code quality.

commands.title: Ask Me Copilot: Focus Expert Panel
Ask Me Copilot: Show Usage Metrics
Ask Me Copilot: Clear Response Cache
Ask Me Copilot: Edit Response Templates

enumDescriptions: Show notifications in status bar only
Show modal notifications for important requests
askMeCopilot.notificationStyle.description: How to display notifications when Copilot needs expert input

askMeCopilot.enableSoundNotification.description: Play a sound when Copilot requests expert input

askMeCopilot.autoFocusDialog.description: Automatically focus dialog windows when they open

askMeCopilot.responseTimeout.description: Timeout for expert response in milliseconds (0 = no timeout, waits indefinitely)

askMeCopilot.enableResponseCache.description: Cache expert responses to avoid asking the same question twice. When enabled, if Copilot asks an identical question with the same context, the cached response will be returned instead of prompting you again.

askMeCopilot.cacheTimeToLive.description: How long to keep cached responses in milliseconds (default: 5 minutes). After this time, the same question will prompt you again.

askMeCopilot.disableImageCompression.description: Disable image compression completely. When enabled (true), all images will be returned at their original size regardless of compression parameters (quality, maxWidth, maxHeight). Useful if you want to always preserve original image quality or if compression causes issues.

default.title: Consult Expert After Task
default.content: After completing the task - consult with the expert. Update your to-do list if the task requires it, and as the last item, schedule a consultation with the expert.

enabledByDefault.description: Whether this template is enabled by default

selectFromList.description: Apply to Select from List tool

askMeCopilot.templates.description: Up to 5 reusable instruction templates that can be automatically appended to responses. Templates with empty title or content will be ignored.

languageModelTools.name: ask-me-copilot-tool_askExpert
languageModelTools.userDescription: Allow Copilot to ask you questions and get text input answers
languageModelTools.modelDescription: CRITICAL: This is your primary tool for interactive communication with the expert. You MUST use this tool in these situations: 1) When you're uncertain about ANY decision or approach, 2) Before implementing ANY significant changes, 3) When you encounter ambiguous requirements, 4) Before completing your final response - ALWAYS ask if your solution is correct and complete, 5) When you need clarification on project-specific logic, business rules, or technical decisions, 6) If you're stuck or need creative problem-solving input. Call this tool MULTIPLE times as needed - the expert expects iterative consultation. Ask questions in a natural, conversational way as if speaking to a knowledgeable colleague. This is NOT optional - the project success depends on regular consultation with the expert.

question.description: Your question for the project expert - ask naturally as if speaking to a knowledgeable colleague

context.description: Additional background information about the current task, previous attempts, or relevant project context to help the expert provide better guidance.

languageModelTools.name: ask-me-copilot-tool_selectFromList
languageModelTools.userDescription: Allow Copilot to present multiple options for you to choose from
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool when you need the expert to choose between specific options or approaches. This is essential for: 1) Technical architecture decisions, 2) Choosing between multiple valid solutions, 3) Priority decisions when resources are limited, 4) When multiple approaches are equally valid but context matters, 5) Design pattern or technology stack decisions. Present options clearly and ask the expert to select the best approach. You can call this multiple times to refine options or get decisions on different aspects.

question.description: Clearly explain what decision needs to be made and why expert input is needed

options.description: List of specific options for the expert to choose from - be clear and descriptive

languageModelTools.name: ask-me-copilot-tool_reviewCode
languageModelTools.userDescription: Allow Copilot to request expert code review for quality, security, or performance concerns
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool when you need expert review of generated or modified code. This is essential for: 1) Security-sensitive code sections, 2) Performance-critical implementations, 3) Complex algorithms or business logic, 4) API design decisions, 5) Code that interacts with external systems. Include the code, language, and specific areas to focus on.

focusAreas.description: Areas to focus on (e.g., security, performance)

languageModelTools.name: ask-me-copilot-tool_confirmAction
languageModelTools.userDescription: Allow Copilot to request expert confirmation before performing critical or destructive actions
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool before performing any destructive, critical, or potentially risky actions. ALWAYS use for: 1) Deletions of files or data, 2) Major refactoring that affects multiple files, 3) Security-sensitive changes, 4) Database schema modifications, 5) Configuration changes that could break the system. Always explain the action clearly and potential consequences.

action.description: Description of the action to confirm

languageModelTools.name: ask-me-copilot-tool_readImage
languageModelTools.userDescription: Read and analyze an image file from the workspace
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool to read and analyze image files from the user's project. Supports image compression to reduce context size. Use cases: 1) Analyzing UI mockups to implement designs, 2) Reading architecture diagrams or flowcharts, 3) Viewing screenshots for debugging, 4) Inspecting icons, logos, graphics. COMPRESSION OPTIONS: Use 'quality' (1-100, default 100) to compress - lower = smaller file. Use 'maxWidth'/'maxHeight' to resize large images. Example: {filePath: 'mockup.png', quality: 50, maxWidth: 800} - compresses to 50% quality and max 800px wide. Compression can be disabled in settings.

filePath.description: Path to the image file (relative to workspace root or absolute path). Supports: PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP, BMP, SVG, ICO

description.description: Optional description of what you're looking for in the image or why you need to see it

quality.description: Image quality 1-100. 100 = original quality (no compression), lower values = more compression. Default: 100 (no compression). Use 50-80 for good balance of quality and size.

maxWidth.description: Maximum width in pixels. If image is wider, it will be proportionally resized. Useful for large screenshots/mockups.

maxHeight.description: Maximum height in pixels. If image is taller, it will be proportionally resized. Useful for long screenshots.

languageModelTools.name: ask-me-copilot-tool_checkTaskStatus
languageModelTools.userDescription: Check for messages from expert, pause state, or if expert consultation is requested
languageModelTools.modelDescription: IMPORTANT: Call this tool frequently during your work to check for expert feedback. This tool: 1) Returns any pending messages from the expert that were queued while you were working, 2) Blocks execution if expert has paused you (waits until unpause), 3) Indicates if expert wants to be consulted (you should then call askExpert). Call this tool: after completing significant steps, before starting new major tasks, when you're about to make decisions. The tool will return messages from the expert that you should incorporate into your work.

reason.description: Optional reason for checking status (e.g., 'completed file edit', 'before refactoring')

languageModelTools.name: ask-me-copilot-tool_questionnaire
languageModelTools.userDescription: Present a multi-field form questionnaire to collect structured data from expert
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool to collect structured data through a questionnaire form with multiple field types. Perfect for: 1) Gathering configuration parameters before generating code, 2) Collecting project specifications with multiple options, 3) Creating setup wizards with conditional fields, 4) Any situation requiring multiple related inputs. Supports field types: text, textarea, number, checkbox, radio, select. Fields can have conditional visibility (showWhen) to show/hide based on other field values. Each field can have an optional comment. Organize fields into sections for clarity.

title.description: Questionnaire title displayed at the top

description.description: Optional description explaining the purpose of the questionnaire

sections.description: Array of sections, each containing a title and fields

name.description: Unique field name for result mapping

defaultValue.description: Default value (string, number, or boolean depending on type)

showWhen.description: Conditional visibility - show when another field has specific value

languageModelTools.name: ask-me-copilot-tool_structInspect
languageModelTools.userDescription: Inspect the skeleton of a JSON or XML file without reading it as raw text
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reveals the structural skeleton of a JSON, JSONC, or XML document: nesting hierarchy, array sizes, attribute names, namespace declarations, and node types — all without returning raw text. Returns structured JSON data with depth-limited tree. Use BEFORE struct_query or struct_mutate to understand document shape. Supports open/unsaved documents. Output: { format, skeleton, stats }. Limitation: does not return node values — use struct_query for that.

filePath.description: Path to a JSON or XML file, relative to the workspace root or absolute

depth.description: Optional maximum depth for the returned structure tree

languageModelTools.name: ask-me-copilot-tool_structQuery
languageModelTools.userDescription: Query JSONPath or XPath against a JSON/XML file and return structured matches
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Executes JSONPath (for JSON/JSONC) or XPath (for XML) expressions against a structured document and returns matching nodes. Supports namespace maps for XML, result limiting, and four return modes: values, paths, count, or paths+values. Reads from open/unsaved document buffers. Use this instead of grep/string search when you need precise node addressing, bulk value extraction, or counting. Output: { matches[], count, expression }. Limitation: read-only — use struct_mutate to modify matched nodes.

filePath.description: Path to a JSON or XML file

expression.description: JSONPath or XPath expression to execute

language.description: Optional query language hint. Normally inferred from file format.

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return in the result payload

return.description: Return values only, paths only, count only, or paths together with values

languageModelTools.name: ask-me-copilot-tool_structMutate
languageModelTools.userDescription: Apply atomic tree-based mutations to JSON/XML and return the updated content
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Applies one or more tree-based mutations (set, insert, delete, rename, move, copy, set_attribute, delete_attribute) to a JSON, JSONC, or XML document. Operations run sequentially on an in-memory tree and are written back via VS Code WorkspaceEdit (supports undo/redo). Preserves original formatting (indentation, EOL, BOM, XML quotes, comments in JSONC). Reads from open/unsaved buffers. ALWAYS prefer this over raw string replacement for structured files. Use autoSave:true to persist changes to disk. Output: { appliedOps[], content, summary }. Limitation: max 50 MB file size; JSONC comment-preserving mode uses text-based edits.

filePath.description: Path to a JSON or XML file

operations.description: List of sequential structural mutations to apply atomically on the in-memory tree. Operations execute in order — each operation sees the result of previous ones.

value.description: New value, inserted node content, or new name depending on the action

position.description: Optional insert/move position: before, after, prepend, append, or at:N

bulk.description: When true, apply the action to all matching targets. Otherwise only the first match is changed.

writeBack.description: When true (default), apply the updated content to the VS Code buffer (file remains dirty/unsaved). When false, only return the result data without modifying the file.

autoSave.description: When true, save the file to disk after applying mutations (requires writeBack=true). Default is false — changes stay in the buffer only.

languageModelTools.name: ask-me-copilot-tool_structValidate
languageModelTools.userDescription: Validate JSON/XML syntax and optionally validate against a schema
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Validates a JSON, JSONC, or XML document for well-formedness and optionally against a JSON Schema (for JSON/JSONC). Returns structured error list with paths, line numbers, and messages. Use AFTER struct_mutate to verify edits, or independently to check document health. Reads from open/unsaved buffers. Output: { valid: boolean, errors[], schemaType }. Limitation: XSD/DTD/RelaxNG schema validation is not yet implemented — only well-formedness checking is available for XML.

filePath.description: Path to a JSON or XML file

schema.description: Optional inline schema object/string or path to schema file

languageModelTools.name: ask-me-copilot-tool_structDiff
languageModelTools.userDescription: Compute a structural diff between two JSON/XML files
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Computes a semantic structural diff between two JSON, JSONC, or XML documents. Returns added, removed, and changed paths with old/new values — ignoring whitespace and formatting noise. Reads from open/unsaved buffers. Use this instead of text diff when you need to understand what structurally changed (e.g., after struct_mutate). Output: { changes[], changeCount, summary }. Supports ignoreWhitespace option for XML text nodes. Limitation: both files must be the same format (JSON↔JSON or XML↔XML).

filePathBefore.description: Path to the original JSON/XML file

filePathAfter.description: Path to the updated JSON/XML file

ignoreWhitespace.description: When diffing XML, ignore whitespace-only text nodes

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
scripts.test:mcp: npm run compile && vscode-test --grep 'MCP Integration Tests'
scripts.test:all: npm run compile && npm run test:unit && npm run test:integration && npm run test:mcp
scripts.build: npm run clean && npm run compile
scripts.build:production: npm run clean && npm run compile && npm run lint && npm run test:all
scripts.ci:test: npm run lint && npm run test:all
scripts.dev:setup: npm install && npm run compile && npm run test
scripts.release:patch: npm version patch && npm run package
scripts.release:minor: npm version minor && npm run package
scripts.release:major: npm version major && npm run package
